title: Supercommando Codex
content: {{Top|leg|notoc=1}} {{Law |relatedevents= |proposedby=[[Jaster Mereel]]{{InsiderCite|80|The History of the Mandalorians: Exploring the Evolution of a Mighty Race of Warriors}} |image= |name=Supercommando Codex |dateproposed=c. [[60 BBY/Legends|60 BBY]]''[[The New Essential Chronology]]'' |government=[[Mandalorian/Legends|Mandalorian]] clans |legislativebody=[[Mandalore (title)/Legends|''Mand'alor'']]''[[Galaxy at War]]'' |result=Mandalorian warriors are held to a progressive new standard of moral behavior |side1=*[[True Mandalorians]] *[[Mandalorian Protectors (Spar)|Mandalorian Protectors]] |side2=*[[Death Watch/Legends|Death Watch]] *[[New Mandalorians/Legends|New Mandalorians]]''[[The Essential Atlas]]'' |otherkeyindividuals= }} {{Quote|The mercenary Jaster Mereel, who became Mandalore in 60 BBY, sought to reinstitute the warrior codes.|[[Vilnau Teupt]], ''[[Industry, Honor, Savagery: Shaping the Mandalorian Soul]]''|''[[The Essential Guide to Warfare]]''}} The '''Supercommando Codex''' was a guideline for [[Mandalorian/Legends|Mandalorian]] behavior, created by the Mandalorian [[Soldier/Legends|soldier]] [[Jaster Mereel]] upon his ascension to the role of [[Mandalore (title)/Legends|''Mand'alor'']] in [[60 BBY/Legends|60 BBY]]. A [[Gender/Legends|man]] of strong ethical principles, Mereel found that many Mandalorians of his era had become dissatisfied with the dishonorable and overly savage ways that had become prevalent among a number of the culture's warriors. Mereel chose to become a reformist, one who sought to restore honor to the clans and reinstitute the old warriors codes that had been largely forgotten over [[Time/Legends|time]]. To that end, Mereel revived and modernized the ancient [[Canons of Honor]] that the [[Mandalorian Crusaders]] and [[Mandalorian Neo-Crusaders/Legends|Neo-Crusaders]] had followed, creating a series of several hundred commandments governing moral Mandalorian behavior, which he entitled the [[Mandalorian Supercommando|Supercommando]] Codex. The Codex, like the Canons before it, drew heavily from the ''[[Resol'nare]]'', the six central tenets of the Mandalorian culture. From the Codex, Mereel preached that any Mandalorians who wished to fight[[Jango Fett: Open Seasons 1|''Jango Fett: Open Seasons'' 1]] would no longer engage in the shameful activities of raiders and brigands. Instead, Mandalorian warriors were to conduct themselves as highly-[[Galactic Credit Standard/Legends|paid]] soldiers and honorable [[Mercenary/Legends|mercenaries]]. Jaster Mereel's reforms were not unanimously approved of, however, and not all Mandalorians agreed to abide by the Supercommando Codex. The [[New Mandalorians/Legends|New Mandalorians]], sometimes described as 'faithless' by [[Tor Vizsla]], rejected participation in the conflict between the supercommandos of the [[True Mandalorians]] and those of the [[Death Watch/Legends|Death Watch]] , after these formations arose following the splintering of the [[Aka'liit]]. They additionally rejected Mereel's reformist warrior codes, and more radical Mandalorians banded together under [[Tor Vizsla]] to violently oppose the Codex and the changes Mereel sought to instill. [[Mandalorian Civil War (True Mandalorians)|Civil war]] broke out between the Death Watch and the [[True Mandalorians|True Mandalorian]] [[Army/Legends|army]] of [[Commando/Legends|supercommandos]] loyal to Mereel. While the True Mandalorians were eventually [[Battle of Galidraan|decimated]] as a result of Death Watch's underhanded machinations, the Death Watch was similarly crippled''[[Imperial Commando: 501st]]'' at the [[Limb/Legends|hands]] of Mereel's surrogate son, [[Jango Fett/Legends|Jango Fett]]. Long after Mereel and his True Mandalorians fell, the tenets of the Supercommando Codex lived on in Mandalorian society. The [[Mandalorian Protectors (Spar)|Mandalorian Protectors]], for instance, drew their moral teachings from the Codex, and strove to adhere to its high standards upon being reformed by the [[Mandalore (title)|Mandalore]] known as [[Spar|The Resurrector]] during the [[Clone Wars/Legends|Clone Wars.]] ==Behind the scenes== Jaster Mereel's code was first alluded to in the [[Jango Fett: Open Seasons 1|first issue]] of the [[Star Wars: Jango Fett: Open Seasons|''Jango Fett: Open Seasons'']] [[Star Wars comic books|comic series]], but it was only with the publication of [[Abel G. Peña]]'s [[2005]] [[Star Wars Insider]] article, [[The History of the Mandalorians: Exploring the Evolution of a Mighty Race of Warriors|''The History of the Mandalorians'']], that the Supercommando Codex was truly introduced to the ''[[Star Wars]]'' universe. The subsequent reference book, ''[[The New Essential Chronology]]'' by [[Daniel Wallace]], briefly mentioned the Supercommando Codex and its role in Mandalorian history, and Wallace would again feature the Codex in his later work in the ''[[Galaxy at War]]'' reference book. The online role-playing guide for the ''[[Star Wars Galaxies]]'' [[Star Wars video games|video game]], entitled ''[https://web.archive.org/web/20100523212919/http://starwarsgalaxies.station.sony.com/players/guides.vm?id=80130 The Way of the Warrior: A Guide to becoming a Mandalorian Soldier]'', also mentioned the Supercommando Codex as an element of a Mandalorian player's potential roleplaying backstory,{{SonyCite|url=players/guides.vm?id=80130|text=The Way of the Warrior: A Guide to becoming a Mandalorian Soldier}} as did ''[[The Essential Guide to Warfare]]'' by [[Jason Fry]] and [[Paul R. Urquhart]]. ==Appearances== *[[Jango Fett: Open Seasons 1|''Jango Fett: Open Seasons'' 1]] {{1stm}} {{Imo}} ==Sources== *{{InsiderCite|80|The History of the Mandalorians: Exploring the Evolution of a Mighty Race of Warriors}} {{1stID|Supercommando Codex}} *''[[The New Essential Chronology]]'' *{{SonyCite|url=players/guides.vm?id=80130|text=The Way of the Warrior: A Guide to becoming a Mandalorian Soldier}} *''[[The Complete Star Wars Encyclopedia]]'' *''[[Galaxy at War]]'' *''[[The Essential Guide to Warfare]]'' {{Imo}} ==Notes and references== {{Reflist}} {{Interlang |de=Superkommando-Kodex |es=Códice Supercomando |pl=Kodeks superkomandosów |pt=Legends:Código Supercomando }} [[Category:Doctrines]] [[Category:Mandalorian laws and acts]]
